dig: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Digo.
din: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Dinka.
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
dinging: verb. present participle and gerund of ding
dining: noun. Eating dinner as a social function.
gid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gidar.
gig: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Goaria.
gin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hinukh.
gird: verb. To bind with a flexible rope or cord.
girding: verb. present participle and gerund of gird
girning: noun. A light-hearted competition in which people girn (make elaborate faces) through a horse collar; most popular in rural parts of
grid: noun. A rectangular array of squares or rectangles of equal size, such as in a crossword puzzle.
griding: verb. present participle and gerund of gride
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
grind: verb. To reduce to smaller pieces by crushing with lateral motion.
grinding: verb. present participle and gerund of grind
indri: noun. One of the largest living lemurs (Indri indri), native to Madagascar.
inn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isinai.
irid: noun. The iris of the eye.
iring: verb. present participle and gerund of ire
rid: verb. Followed by of: to free (oneself or someone, or a place) from an annoyance or hindrance.
riding: verb. present participle and gerund of ride
rig: noun. The rigging of a sailing ship or other such craft.
rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
ringing: noun. The sound of something that rings.
dirging: verb. present participle and gerund of dirge
ggr: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Aghu Tharnggalu.
ging: noun. A company; troop; a gang.
igg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Igana.
indin: noun. A dark red crystalline substance, isomeric with and resembling indigo blue, and obtained from isatide and dioxindol.
ing: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Deg Xinag.
nid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandi.
nig: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngalakan.
rinding: verb. present participle and gerund of rind
